creator: Sarah Perez
title: (No AI) Steven Spielberg says he’s ‘never used AI’ in any of his films
dc:creator: Sarah Perez
content: At SXSW, Steven Spielberg said AI has uses in many fields, but not when it comes to replacing creative people in film and TV writing.
contentSnippet: At SXSW, Steven Spielberg said AI has uses in many fields, but not when it comes to replacing creative people in film and TV writing.

title: (No AI) Facebook Marketplace adds AI auto-replies for annoying ‘Is this still available?’ messages
author: Emma Roth
content:
![An image of the Facebook logo surrounded by blue circles](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2025/04/STK040_VRG_Illo_N_Barclay_2_facebook.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Facebook Marketplace is [adding a bunch of new AI-powered tools](https://about.fb.com/news/2026/03/facebook-marketplace-new-meta-ai-tools-make-selling-faster-and-easier/) that are supposed to make selling items on the platform a little more efficient. One feature will use Meta AI to automatically respond to those annoying "Is this still available?" messages.

You can toggle on the auto-reply option when creating a listing, and Meta AI will draft an editable response to any questions related to availability. In an example shown by Meta, its AI assistant creates an auto-reply saying, "Yes, it's still available. Do you have any questions?"

![](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/03/auto-reply-fb.webp?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Facebook Marketplace is trying to speed up the process of listing items, too, as Meta AI can now use the photos …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/tech/893907/facebook-marketplace-ai-auto-reply-listings)
contentSnippet:
Facebook Marketplace is adding a bunch of new AI-powered tools that are supposed to make selling items on the platform a little more efficient. One feature will use Meta AI to automatically respond to those annoying "Is this still available?" messages.
You can toggle on the auto-reply option when creating a listing, and Meta AI will draft an editable response to any questions related to availability. In an example shown by Meta, its AI assistant creates an auto-reply saying, "Yes, it's still available. Do you have any questions?"
Facebook Marketplace is trying to speed up the process of listing items, too, as Meta AI can now use the photos …
Read the full story at The Verge.

title: (No AI) Gemini’s task automation is here and it’s wild
author: Allison Johnson
content:
![Galaxy S26 Ultra phone showing Gemini task automation interface](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/03/dsc03087_processed.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Computers ordering cappuccinos.

A couple of weeks ago, [Google and Samsung announced a big Gemini development](https://www.theverge.com/tech/884210/google-gemini-samsung-s26-pixel-10-uber) coming to their newest devices: task automation. Starting with food delivery and rideshare apps, Gemini would be able to use certain apps on your behalf in a virtual window to take care of things like ordering dinner or getting a car to the airport - all based on simple prompts. You know, [all the stuff that we've been promised](https://www.theverge.com/tech/884703/google-samsung-galaxy-s26-gemini-apple-siri) for years AI assistants will be able to do. That feature wasn't live when I first started testing the S26 Ultra, but it just arrived in beta as part of an update. And boy is it weird watching your phone use itself!

The first prompt I gave it …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/tech/893820/gemini-task-automation-samsung-s26-google-pixel-10)

creator: Reece Rogers
title: (No AI) Google's AI Search Results Love to Refer You Back to Google
dc:creator: Reece Rogers

creator: Zeyi Yang
title: (No AI) China’s OpenClaw Boom Is a Gold Rush for AI Companies
dc:creator: Zeyi Yang
content: Hype around the open source agent is driving people to rent cloud servers and buy AI subscriptions just to try it, creating a windfall for tech companies.
contentSnippet: Hype around the open source agent is driving people to rent cloud servers and buy AI subscriptions just to try it, creating a windfall for tech companies.
